project.name: LLM Gateway
project.description: LLM API security proxy gateway — intercepts, scans, and audits all OpenAI-compatible API requests

archived_sprint.name: Phase 8 - Admin Playwright E2E Testing & Bug Audit
archived_sprint.goal: Implement comprehensive Playwright E2E test suite for the admin dashboard (8 spec files, ~46 tests) using a real backend server, and generate a structured bug list (BUGS.md) documenting all discovered functional, accessibility, and code quality issues.

features.title: Install Playwright and create test configuration
features.description: Install @playwright/test as a devDependency in admin/package.json. Create admin/playwright.config.ts with Chromium browser, baseURL pointing to http://localhost:3000, webServer configuration to start the backend, test match pattern admin/e2e/**/*.spec.ts, and screenshot/video artifacts output. Add test:e2e and test:e2e:ui scripts to admin/package.json. Install Playwright browsers via npx playwright install chromium.
acceptance_criteria: Given @playwright/test is installed, when npx playwright test --config admin/playwright.config.ts runs, then the test runner starts without errors
Given playwright.config.ts webServer config, when tests run, then the backend server starts automatically
Given admin/package.json, when pnpm test:e2e is called, then it invokes playwright test with the correct config
features.technical_notes: Playwright config should set baseURL to http://localhost:3000/admin/ since the admin SPA is served at /admin. Use webServer.command to start the backend with a test config. Set timeout to 30s for E2E tests. Use { channel: 'chromium' } for browser. Set use.screenshot to 'only-on-failure', use.video to 'retain-on-failure'. Artifacts output to admin/e2e/results/.

features.title: Create E2E test fixtures with real backend server
features.description: Create admin/e2e/fixtures/admin-server.ts that starts a real Fastify server with in-memory SQLite, a known admin_token (e.g. 'test-admin-token'), a test provider config, and helper methods to create test keys, seed audit logs, and get the authenticated browser state. Create admin/e2e/fixtures/index.ts extending Playwright's base test with custom fixtures (authenticatedPage, adminToken, createKey, seedAuditLog). Create admin/e2e/global-setup.ts to build the admin frontend before tests.
acceptance_criteria: Given the fixture, when tests use the 'authenticatedPage' fixture, then the browser has a valid admin token in localStorage and is logged in
Given the server fixture, when it starts, then a Fastify instance with in-memory SQLite and test config is running on a dynamic port
Given createKey helper, when called, then a test API key is created via the real backend and the key data is returned
Given seedAuditLog helper, when called with log data, then an audit log entry is inserted into the test database
features.technical_notes: Reuse the pattern from tests/helpers/setup.ts (createTestServer) but adapt for Playwright. The fixture should: (1) Build admin/dist if not exists (global-setup), (2) Start Fastify server on port 3000 with config.admin_token='test-admin-token', in-memory SQLite, and a mock provider, (3) Provide authenticatedPage fixture that navigates to /admin/ and sets localStorage token, (4) Provide createKey() that calls POST /admin/keys, (5) Provide seedAuditLog() that directly inserts into the DB. Server lifecycle: start in fixture setup, stop in teardown.

features.title: E2E tests for Login page (~8 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/login.spec.ts with ~8 tests covering the login page: renders login form with title and token input, shows/hide password toggle works, login with valid admin token redirects to overview page, login with invalid token shows error message, submitting empty token shows validation error, already-authenticated user visiting /login gets redirected to overview, auth guard redirects unauthenticated users to /login, logout clears token and redirects to login.
acceptance_criteria: Given /admin/login page, when rendered, then card with 'LLM Gateway Admin' title and password input is visible
Given the password input, when Eye/EyeOff toggle is clicked, then input type switches between password and text
Given valid admin token entered, when form is submitted, then page redirects to /admin/ overview
Given invalid token entered, when form is submitted, then error message is displayed
Given no token entered, when form is submitted, then validation prevents submission
Given authenticated user, when navigating to /admin/login, then redirect to /admin/ occurs
Given unauthenticated user, when accessing /admin/keys, then redirect to /admin/login occurs
Given authenticated user, when clicking Logout, then token is cleared and redirect to /admin/login occurs
features.technical_notes: Use authenticatedPage fixture from s8-feat-002 for tests that need logged-in state. For invalid token tests, use a fresh page without token. Selectors: input[type='password'] or placeholder text, Eye/EyeOff button, submit button, error message text. Verify redirect via page.url(). Test the auth guard by navigating to a protected route and checking the final URL.

features.title: E2E tests for Navigation and layout (~5 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/navigation.spec.ts with ~5 tests: sidebar navigation links work (each link navigates to correct page), sidebar collapse/expand toggle works on desktop, mobile hamburger menu opens sidebar overlay, active nav item is highlighted, page header displays correct title for each route.
acceptance_criteria: Given the sidebar, when Home/Keys/Audit/Security/Providers/Settings links are clicked, then correct page loads
Given the sidebar toggle button, when clicked, then sidebar collapses to icon-only mode (64px width)
Given the sidebar collapsed, when toggle is clicked again, then sidebar expands back to full width (240px)
Given viewport < 768px, when hamburger menu is clicked, then sidebar appears as overlay
Given current route is /admin/keys, when sidebar renders, then the Keys link shows active/highlighted state
features.technical_notes: Use authenticatedPage fixture. Test each sidebar link by clicking and verifying URL change and page content. For collapse test, check element width or class changes. For mobile test, use page.setViewportSize({width: 375, height: 667}). Active state can be verified by checking for an active CSS class or aria-current attribute.

features.title: E2E tests for Overview Dashboard page (~5 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/overview.spec.ts with ~5 tests: page loads and displays 4 KPI cards (Total Requests, Token Usage, Total Cost, Avg Latency), bar chart for Requests by Model renders, pie chart for Requests by Status renders, recent activity table shows rows, donut chart for PII Detection Rate renders. Seed test audit data before running tests.
acceptance_criteria: Given seeded audit data, when overview page loads, then 4 KPI cards display correct formatted values
Given audit stats with byModel data, when bar chart renders, then chart container is visible with axis labels
Given audit stats with byStatus data, when pie chart renders, then chart container is visible with legend
Given seeded recent logs, when recent activity table renders, then table rows with model, tokens, cost, status are visible
Given piiDetectionRate from stats, when donut chart renders, then percentage text is displayed
features.technical_notes: Seed audit logs using seedAuditLog helper with varied data (different models, statuses, PII scores). KPI card selectors: look for formatted numbers in card elements. Chart verification: check that recharts SVG containers exist and have rendered content (not just empty). Table verification: check row count matches seeded data (limited to 5). Use page.waitForSelector for chart SVG elements since they render asynchronously.

features.title: E2E tests for API Key Management page (~10 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/keys.spec.ts with ~10 tests: keys table loads and displays columns, create key dialog opens and submits successfully, created key is shown in display dialog with copy button, edit key sheet opens with pre-filled data, edit key name updates successfully, edit key rate limits updates successfully, revoke key shows confirmation dialog, revoke key marks key as revoked, revoked keys show disabled edit button, pagination controls work for multiple pages of keys.
acceptance_criteria: Given existing keys, when keys page loads, then table displays rows with Name, Prefix, Rate Limits, Created, Status columns
Given 'Create Key' button clicked, when dialog opens, then name input and RPM/TPM/RPD fields are visible
Given create form filled with name, when submitted, then key display dialog shows the generated gwk_ key with copy button
Given a key row, when edit (pencil) icon is clicked, then side sheet opens with pre-filled name and rate limits
Given edit sheet with modified name, when saved, then key name updates in the table
Given a key row, when revoke (trash) icon is clicked, then confirmation dialog appears with destructive button
Given confirmation dialog accepted, when revoke completes, then key status changes to 'Revoked' in the table
Given a revoked key, when its row renders, then edit button is disabled
Given >20 keys exist, when pagination next is clicked, then next page of keys loads with correct offset
Given copy button in key display dialog, when clicked, then key is copied (verify clipboard or checkmark feedback)
features.technical_notes: Create multiple keys via createKey helper for pagination test (>20 keys). Test the full CRUD lifecycle: create -> verify in table -> edit -> verify update -> revoke -> verify revoked. Key display dialog: look for 'gwk_' prefix in text content. Edit sheet: verify it slides in from right side. Copy button: check for checkmark icon change after click. Revoke: verify the red 'Revoke' button in confirmation dialog.

features.title: E2E tests for Audit Log Browser page (~8 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/audit.spec.ts with ~8 tests: audit table loads with default view, filter by status dropdown works, filter by model dropdown works, date range filter works, clicking a row opens detail drawer, detail drawer displays all log fields, detail drawer can be closed, CSV export triggers a file download, pagination controls work.
acceptance_criteria: Given seeded audit logs, when audit page loads, then log table displays rows with Time, Request ID, Model, Tokens, Cost, Latency, Status columns
Given status filter dropdown, when 'blocked' is selected, then only blocked logs are shown in table
Given model filter dropdown, when a specific model is selected, then only matching logs are shown
Given date range inputs, when a range is set, then only logs within range are shown
Given a log row, when clicked, then detail drawer slides in from the right
Given detail drawer open, when content is checked, then Request ID, Timestamp, Status, Model, Tokens, Cost, Latency, PII info, Injection Score fields are visible
Given detail drawer open, when close button or backdrop is clicked, then drawer closes
Given export button, when clicked, then a CSV file download is triggered (verify download event)
features.technical_notes: Seed varied audit logs: different statuses (success, error, blocked), different models, different dates. For filter tests: apply filter and verify table row count changes. Detail drawer: check for Sheet/drawer container appearing on the right side. CSV export: use page.waitForEvent('download') to catch the download. Pagination: seed >50 logs to test pagination.

features.title: E2E tests for Security Monitor page (~4 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/security.spec.ts with ~4 tests: page loads and displays 4 security KPI cards (Blocked Requests, PII Detections, Injection Attempts, Content Filter), PII Detection by Type bar chart renders, Injection Score Distribution histogram renders, Threat Feed table shows blocked request entries.
acceptance_criteria: Given seeded security data (blocked logs, PII, injection scores), when security page loads, then 4 KPI cards display values
Given PII detection by type data, when bar chart renders, then horizontal bar chart is visible with PII type labels
Given injection score distribution data, when histogram renders, then 5 score bucket bars are visible
Given blocked audit logs, when threat feed table renders, then rows show Time, Request ID, Model, PII badge, Injection Score
features.technical_notes: Seed audit logs with PII data (pii_types_found with EMAIL, PHONE etc.), injection scores, and blocked status. KPI cards: verify formatted numbers are present. Charts: verify SVG containers rendered. Threat feed: verify table rows with blocked status.

features.title: E2E tests for Providers page (~3 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/providers.spec.ts with ~3 tests: provider cards render with name and details, health indicator dots display correct colors, key health bars are visible in each provider card.
acceptance_criteria: Given configured providers, when providers page loads, then provider cards display with name, baseUrl, key strategy, key count
Given provider health data, when cards render, then health dots (green/yellow/red/gray) are visible
Given provider with multiple keys, when key health section renders, then key health bars are visible with latency data
features.technical_notes: Providers come from the test server config (config.toml). Health tracker may have no data in tests — verify cards still render with default health states. Look for provider card containers and health dot elements.

features.title: E2E tests for Settings page (~3 tests)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/settings.spec.ts with ~3 tests: page loads and displays config sections (General, Security, Retry), provider configuration section shows provider details, TOML preview code block renders with syntax-highlighted content.
acceptance_criteria: Given the config API returns data, when settings page loads, then General (port, host, log_level, rate limits), Security (injection threshold, PII types), and Retry (max_retries, delays) sections are visible
Given provider data, when provider section renders, then provider cards with base URL, key strategy, key count are shown
Given the TOML preview section, when rendered, then a code block with monospace font and TOML-formatted text is visible
features.technical_notes: Config sections are read-only. Verify section headings (General, Security, Retry Policy) and key-value pairs. TOML preview: look for a pre/code element with TOML-formatted text. Provider section: may be combined with main provider list or a separate config view.

features.title: Generate comprehensive bug list (BUGS.md)
features.description: Create admin/e2e/BUGS.md documenting all discovered bugs from code analysis and E2E testing. Categorize into: Functional Bugs (latency delta color inverted, CSV export no error UI, export memory risk, clipboard API failure, rate limit 0 default, pie chart color mismatch, hardcoded empty state, formatUsd dead code, provider health bar misleading width), Accessibility Bugs (missing label associations, no focus traps, no Escape key close, missing aria-labels), and Code Quality Issues (unused dependencies, duplicated components, naming confusion). Each bug entry should include: severity, location, description, steps to reproduce, and expected vs actual behavior.
acceptance_criteria: Given all E2E tests have run, when bug list is generated, then BUGS.md contains all discovered issues
Given the bug list, when reviewed, then each bug has severity (high/medium/low), location (file:line), description, and reproduction steps
Given the 16 pre-analysis bugs, when documented, then all are present in BUGS.md with accurate details
Given any new bugs found during E2E testing, when documented, then they are added to BUGS.md
features.technical_notes: Pre-identified bugs from code analysis (16 bugs): Functional (9): 1) Latency delta color inverted in overview.tsx, 2) CSV export no error UI in audit.tsx, 3) Export memory risk in export.ts, 4) Clipboard API no try/catch in keys.tsx, 5) Rate limit defaults of 0 in create-dialog/edit-sheet, 6) Pie chart legend color mismatch in pie-chart.tsx, 7) Hardcoded 'No keys found' in data-table.tsx, 8) formatUsd dead code in utils.ts, 9) Provider health bar misleading width. Accessibility (4): 10) Missing htmlFor/id on labels, 11) No focus traps in modals, 12) No Escape key close, 13) Missing aria-labels. Code Quality (3): 14) Unused deps (date-fns, class-variance-authority), 15) Duplicated StatusBadge, 16) Duplicated InjectionScoreBar. During E2E testing, look for additional runtime bugs like broken navigation, missing data rendering, incorrect form submissions, etc.
